sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click the yellow/orange square at (16, 50) again
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 50}])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
g_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g']
print(f"After clicking (16, 50): g={len(g_nodes)}")
for n in g_nodes:
    center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])//len(n['boundary']), sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])//len(n['boundary']))
    print(f"  g at {center}, hash={n['hash']}")

# Check if the g at (0, 6) is a timer by checking its hash over time
# Let me also check all nodes to understand the board better
all_nodes = seg['nodes']
print(f"\nTotal nodes: {len(all_nodes)}")
colors = {}
for n in all_nodes:
    c = n['color']
    if c not in colors:
        colors[c] = []
    center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])//len(n['boundary']), sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])//len(n['boundary']))
    colors[c].append((center, n['hash'], n['pixels']))
for c, items in sorted(colors.items()):
    print(f"  {c}: {len(items)} nodes")
    for item in items:
        print(f"    at {item[0]}, hash={item[1]}, pixels={item[2]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
